devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । सनकाद्या नारदश्च ऋभुर्हंसोऽरुणिर्यतिः । नैते गृहान् ब्रह्मसुता ह्यावसन् ऊर्ध्वरेतसः ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: maitreya uvācasanakādyā nāradaśca ṛbhurhaṃso'ruṇiryatiḥnaite gṛhān brahmasutā hyāvasan ūrdhvaretasaḥ
translation:
Maitreya said:
Sanaka and others (viz. Sanandana, Sanātana and Sanatkumāra), Nārada, Ṛbhu, Haṃsa, Aruṇa and Yati—these sons of Brahmā did not take to the life of a householder, and observed life-long celibacy.

devanagari: मृषाधर्मस्य भार्यासीद् दम्भं मायां च शत्रुहन् । असूत मिथुनं तत्तु निर्ऋतिर्जगृहेऽप्रजः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: mṛṣādharmasya bhāryāsīd dambhaṃ māyāṃ ca śatruhanasūta mithunaṃ tattu nirṛtirjagṛhe'prajaḥ
translation: Oh Vidura (destroyer of enemies), Adharma’s wife, by name Mṛṣā (fakehood) gave birth to a twin—Dambha (Hypocrisy) and Māyā (deceit). Nirṛti (the Evil spirit presiding over the South-West) who was childless adopted that couple (as Dambha married his sister Māyā).

devanagari: तयोः समभवल्लोभो निकृतिश्च महामते । ताभ्यां क्रोधश्च हिंसा च यद्दुरुक्तिः स्वसा कलिः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: tayoḥ samabhavallobho nikṛtiśca mahāmatetābhyāṃ krodhaśca hiṃsā ca yadduruktiḥ svasā kaliḥ
translation: Oh man of great wisdom (Vidura)! To them (Dambha and Māyā) were born Lobha (Avarice) and Nikṛti (Wickedness). Of these (Lobha and Nikṛti) were born Krodha (Anger) and Hiṃsā (Violence); of these again (were born) Kali (Quarrel) and his sister Durukti (Abusive language).

devanagari: दुरुक्तौ कलिराधत्त भयं मृत्युं च सत्तम । तयोश्च मिथुनं जज्ञे यातना निरयस्तथा ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: duruktau kalirādhatta bhayaṃ mṛtyuṃ ca sattamatayośca mithunaṃ jajñe yātanā nirayastathā
translation: Kali begot Bhaya (Fear) and (the daughter) Mṛtyu (Death) through Durukti. Of this couple (Bhaya and Mṛtyu) was born a twin, viz. Niraya (Hell) and Yātanā (Extreme tortures), Oh righteous-most Vidura.

devanagari: सङ्ग्रहेण मयाऽख्यातः प्रतिसर्गस्तवानघ । त्रिः श्रुत्वैतत्पुमान् पुण्यं विधुनोत्यात्मनो मलम् ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: saṅgraheṇa mayā'khyātaḥ pratisargastavānaghatriḥ śrutvaitatpumān puṇyaṃ vidhunotyātmano malam
translation: Oh sinless one! I have described to you briefly the Pratisarga (secondary creation or the creation that leads to Pralaya). Having heard this sacred account thrice, one shakes off the impurities from his Soul.

devanagari: अथातः कीर्तये वंशं पुण्यकीर्तेः कुरूद्वह । स्वायम्भुवस्यापि मनोः हरेरंशांशजन्मनः ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: athātaḥ kīrtaye vaṃśaṃ puṇyakīrteḥ kurūdvahasvāyambhuvasyāpi manoḥ hareraṃśāṃśajanmanaḥ
translation: Oh Scion of Kuru! I shall now describe the race of Svāyambhuva Manu of sacred renown, born from the part of (Brahmā who himself was) Hari’s aṃśa (part).

devanagari: प्रियव्रतोत्तानपादौ शतरूपापतेः सुतौ । वासुदेवस्य कलया रक्षायां जगतः स्थितौ ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: priyavratottānapādau śatarūpāpateḥ sutauvāsudevasya kalayā rakṣāyāṃ jagataḥ sthitau
translation: Priyavrata and Uttānapāda, the sons of Manu, the consort of Śatarūpā, were engaged in the protection of the world, as they were endowed with an aṃśa (a part of protective energy) of Lord Vāsudeva.

devanagari: जाये उत्तानपादस्य सुनीतिः सुरुचिस्तयोः । सुरुचिः प्रेयसी पत्युः नेतरा यत्सुतो ध्रुवः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: jāye uttānapādasya sunītiḥ surucistayoḥsuruciḥ preyasī patyuḥ netarā yatsuto dhruvaḥ
translation: Uttānapāda had two wives—Sunīti and Suruci. (Of the two) Suruci was more beloved of her lord, and not the other (Sunīti) whose son was Dhruva.

devanagari: एकदा सुरुचेः पुत्रं अङ्कमारोप्य लालयन् । उत्तमं नारुरुक्षन्तं ध्रुवं राजाभ्यनन्दत ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: ekadā suruceḥ putraṃ aṅkamāropya lālayanuttamaṃ nārurukṣantaṃ dhruvaṃ rājābhyanandata
translation: One day, while the king was fondling Suruci’s son Uttama by placing him on his lap, he did not like Dhruva who also wished to climb up his (Uttānapāda’s) lap.

devanagari: तथा चिकीर्षमाणं तं सपत्‍न्यास्तनयं ध्रुवम् । सुरुचिः शृण्वतो राज्ञः सेर्ष्यमाहातिगर्विता ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: tathā cikīrṣamāṇaṃ taṃ sapat‍nyāstanayaṃ dhruvamsuruciḥ śṛṇvato rājñaḥ serṣyamāhātigarvitā
translation: To Dhruva, her co-wife’s son who was thus trying to climb (his father’s lap), the extremely proud Suruci jealously spoke, while the king was listening:

devanagari: न वत्स नृपतेर्धिष्ण्यं भवान् आरोढुमर्हति । न गृहीतो मया यत्त्वं कुक्षौ अपि नृपात्मजः ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: na vatsa nṛpaterdhiṣṇyaṃ bhavān āroḍhumarhatina gṛhīto mayā yattvaṃ kukṣau api nṛpātmajaḥ
translation: ‘Oh child, even though you are the king’s son, you do not deserve to ascend the royal throne, as you were not borne by me, in my womb.

devanagari: बालोऽसि बत नात्मानं अन्यस्त्रीगर्भसम्भृतम् । नूनं वेद भवान् यस्य दुर्लभेऽर्थे मनोरथः ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: bālo'si bata nātmānaṃ anyastrīgarbhasambhṛtamnūnaṃ veda bhavān yasya durlabhe'rthe manorathaḥ
translation: It is a pity that you are but a child, and you do not verily know that you were borne in another woman’s womb. Hence you desire for an unattainable object.

devanagari: तपसाऽराध्य पुरुषं तस्यैवानुग्रहेण मे । गर्भे त्वं साधयात्मानं यदीच्छसि नृपासनम् ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: tapasā'rādhya puruṣaṃ tasyaivānugraheṇa megarbhe tvaṃ sādhayātmānaṃ yadīcchasi nṛpāsanam
translation: If you cherish a desire for the king’s throne, you propitiate the Lord (Puruṣa) by penance, and by His grace, get yourself borne in my womb (secure birth as my son).”

devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । मातुः सपत्‍न्याः स दुरुक्तिविद्धः । श्वसन् रुषा दण्डहतो यथाहिः । हित्वा मिषन्तं पितरं सन्नवाचं । जगाम मातुः प्ररुदन् सकाश...
english_devnagari: maitreya uvācamātuḥ sapat‍nyāḥ sa duruktividdhaḥśvasan ruṣā daṇḍahato yathāhiḥhitvā miṣantaṃ pitaraṃ sannavācaṃjagāma mātuḥ prarudan sakāśam
translation:
Maitreya said:
Being cut to the quick by the harsh words of his stepmother, Dhruva, like a serpent beaten with a stick, heaved heavy sighs with anger. Leaving his father who was merely looking on tongue-tied, he went away crying to his mother.

devanagari: तं निःश्वसन्तं स्फुरिताधरोष्ठं । सुनीतिरुत्सङ्ग उदूह्य बालम् । निशम्य तत्पौरमुखान्नितान्तं । सा विव्यथे यद्‍गदितं सपत्‍न्या ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ niḥśvasantaṃ sphuritādharoṣṭhaṃsunītirutsaṅga udūhya bālamniśamya tatpauramukhānnitāntaṃsā vivyathe yad‍gaditaṃ sapat‍nyā
translation: Sunīti took up on her lap, the child which was (heavily) sighing with quivering lips. She was extremely distressed to hear from the inmates of the harem, the words of her co-wife.

devanagari: सोत्सृज्य धैर्यं विललाप शोक । दावाग्निना दावलतेव बाला । वाक्यं सपत्‍न्याः स्मरती सरोज । श्रिया दृशा बाष्पकलामुवाह ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: sotsṛjya dhairyaṃ vilalāpa śokadāvāgninā dāvalateva bālāvākyaṃ sapat‍nyāḥ smaratī sarojaśriyā dṛśā bāṣpakalāmuvāha
translation: Being scorched by grief like a forest creeper by the (encircling) forest conflagration, the young queen lost her heart and wept bitterly. Remembering the words of her cowife, tears flowed out from her lotuslike beautiful eyes.

devanagari: दीर्घं श्वसन्ती वृजिनस्य पारं । अपश्यती बालकमाह बाला । मामङ्गलं तात परेषु मंस्था । भुङ्क्ते जनो यत्परदुःखदस्तत् ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: dīrghaṃ śvasantī vṛjinasya pāraṃapaśyatī bālakamāha bālāmāmaṅgalaṃ tāta pareṣu maṃsthābhuṅkte jano yatparaduḥkhadastat
translation: Heaving long sighs, and being unable to see the end of the misery (miserable state), the young lady spoke to her child, “Oh child! Do not blame others for the misery. A person experiences the same suffering which he has inflicted on others.

devanagari: सत्यं सुरुच्याभिहितं भवान्मे । यद् दुर्भगाया उदरे गृहीतः । स्तन्येन वृद्धश्च विलज्जते यां । भार्येति वा वोढुमिडस्पतिर्माम् ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: satyaṃ surucyābhihitaṃ bhavānmeyad durbhagāyā udare gṛhītaḥstanyena vṛddhaśca vilajjate yāṃbhāryeti vā voḍhumiḍaspatirmām
translation: What Suruci said was true as you were borne in the womb of and were brought up. with the milk of my unfortunate self whom the king is ashamed of recognising as his wife (nay even his maid servant).

devanagari: आतिष्ठ तत्तात विमत्सरस्त्वं । उक्तं समात्रापि यदव्यलीकम् । आराधयाधोक्षजपादपद्मं । यदीच्छसेऽध्यासनमुत्तमो यथा ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: ātiṣṭha tattāta vimatsarastvaṃuktaṃ samātrāpi yadavyalīkamārādhayādhokṣajapādapadmaṃyadīcchase'dhyāsanamuttamo yathā
translation: Oh child! Remain above jealousy. Abide by the true path even though it is advised by your step-mother. If you desire the highest seat (throne) like Uttama, propitiate the feet of Adhokṣaja (god Viṣṇu).

devanagari: यस्याङ्घ्रिपद्मं परिचर्य विश्व । विभावनायात्तगुणाभिपत्तेः । अजोऽध्यतिष्ठत्खलु पारमेष्ठ्यं । पदं जितात्मश्वसनाभिवन्द्यम् ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: yasyāṅghripadmaṃ paricarya viśvavibhāvanāyāttaguṇābhipatteḥajo'dhyatiṣṭhatkhalu pārameṣṭhyaṃpadaṃ jitātmaśvasanābhivandyam
translation: It is by worshipping the lotuslike feet of Lord Viṣṇu who has assumed the sattva guṇa for the protection of the universe, that god Brahmā attained to the position of parameṣṭhin (the most exalted among gods), which is worthy of being bowed to by sages, who have controlled their minds and breath.

devanagari: तथा मनुर्वो भगवान्पितामहो । यमेकमत्या पुरुदक्षिणैर्मखैः । इष्ट्वाभिपेदे दुरवापमन्यतो । भौमं सुखं दिव्यमथापवर्ग्यम् ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: tathā manurvo bhagavānpitāmahoyamekamatyā purudakṣiṇairmakhaiḥiṣṭvābhipede duravāpamanyatobhaumaṃ sukhaṃ divyamathāpavargyam
translation: So also, your grandfather, the venerable Manu, with complete faith in him as the antaryāmin of all, worshipped him, by performing sacrifices with liberal sacrificial fees (dakṣiṇā). He obtained earthly and heavenly happiness, and the highest bliss in Mokṣa, unattainable by other means.

devanagari: तमेव वत्साश्रय भृत्यवत्सलं । मुमुक्षुभिर्मृग्यपदाब्जपद्धतिम् । अनन्यभावे निजधर्मभाविते । मनस्यवस्थाप्य भजस्व पूरुषम् ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: tameva vatsāśraya bhṛtyavatsalaṃmumukṣubhirmṛgyapadābjapaddhatimananyabhāve nijadharmabhāvitemanasyavasthāpya bhajasva pūruṣam
translation: Oh child, resort to him who is kind to his servants (devotees), the path of whose lotuslike feet should be sought by people desirous of Liberation (Mokṣa). Adore the Puruṣa (Supreme Man) by firmly fixing him in your mind by being exclusively devoted to him, and purified by performance of righteous duties.

devanagari: नान्यं ततः पद्मपलाशलोचनाद् । दुःखच्छिदं ते मृगयामि कञ्चन । यो मृग्यते हस्तगृहीतपद्मया । श्रियेतरैरङ्ग विमृग्यमाणया ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: nānyaṃ tataḥ padmapalāśalocanādduḥkhacchidaṃ te mṛgayāmi kañcanayo mṛgyate hastagṛhītapadmayāśriyetarairaṅga vimṛgyamāṇayā
translation: My child! I find none other than the lotus-eyed God who can redress your grievance. The Lord is sought after by goddess Lakṣmī with a lotus in her hand, but who herself is sought by all others (like god Brahmā etc.).

devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । एवं सञ्जल्पितं मातुः आकर्ण्यार्थागमं वचः । सन्नियम्यात्मनाऽत्मानं निश्चक्राम पितुः पुरात् ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: maitreya uvācaevaṃ sañjalpitaṃ mātuḥ ākarṇyārthāgamaṃ vacaḥsanniyamyātmanā'tmānaṃ niścakrāma pituḥ purāt
translation:
Maitreya said:
Having heard the words thus spoken out to him, guiding him how to attain his objective, Dhruva controlled his mind by his will power, and left his father’s capital.

devanagari: नारदस्तदुपाकर्ण्य ज्ञात्वा तस्य चिकीर्षितम् । स्पृष्ट्वा मूर्धन्यघघ्नेन पाणिना प्राह विस्मितः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: nāradastadupākarṇya jñātvā tasya cikīrṣitamspṛṣṭvā mūrdhanyaghaghnena pāṇinā prāha vismitaḥ
translation: When Nārada heard this and came to know what he (Dhruva) desired to do, he touched his (Dhruva’s) head with his purifying hand, and said to himself in wonder:

devanagari: अहो तेजः क्षत्रियाणां मानभङ्गममृष्यताम् । बालोऽप्ययं हृदा धत्ते यत्समातुरसद्वचः ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: aho tejaḥ kṣatriyāṇāṃ mānabhaṅgamamṛṣyatāmbālo'pyayaṃ hṛdā dhatte yatsamāturasadvacaḥ
translation:
“Oh, how wonderful is the spirit of the Kṣatriyas who cannot put up with the loss of respect; for even though (Dhruva) is but a child, he has taken to heart, the vile speech of the step-mother.”
26(A). “Oh child! Where are you going alone leaving behind your home full of prosperity? I can perceive, Oh child, that you are indignant at the insulting treatment (meted out to you) by your own relatives.”
26(B). “Did you perceive this, Sir, by your power of contemplation developed by Yoga?[2] I cannot console my heart which is wounded by the words of Suruci.”
purport:
Footnote [2]:
You have seen everything by your Yogic power born of contemplation. Although you can perceive everything, I, however, tell it to you.—Padaratnāvalī

devanagari: नारद उवाच । नाधुनाप्यवमानं ते सम्मानं वापि पुत्रक । लक्षयामः कुमारस्य सक्तस्य क्रीडनादिषु ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: nārada uvācanādhunāpyavamānaṃ te sammānaṃ vāpi putrakalakṣayāmaḥ kumārasya saktasya krīḍanādiṣu
translation:
Nārada said:
Oh child, we do not find that (the sense of) honour or dishonour is developed now (at this early stage) in a child (like you), who is (normally and) naturally fond of play and (such) other things.

devanagari: विकल्पे विद्यमानेऽपि न ह्यसन्तोषहेतवः । पुंसो मोहमृते भिन्ना यल्लोके निजकर्मभिः ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: vikalpe vidyamāne'pi na hyasantoṣahetavaḥpuṃso mohamṛte bhinnā yalloke nijakarmabhiḥ
translation: [3] Even though the sense of distinction between honour and dishonour is developed, there are no other causes of dissatisfaction and misery for a man except his misapprehension. For in this world, people are different in their weal and woe as a result of their (past) karmas.
purport:
Footnote [3]:
(1) VC: Insults or those who inflict them (upon us) are the creations of our misapprehension. From the point of jñāna-yogins, the vikalpa (the difference between honour and dishonour) being non-existent, the misery (resulting from mal-treatment) or the persons who cause it have no existence. In the case of bhaktas (bhakti-yogins) and karma-yogins, they consider that as everything in this world happens according to our past karmas, they should not blame anybody else except their own fate.
(2) In the first half Bhāgavata Candrikā prefers to read vikalpe avidya-māne'pi instead of vidyamane'pi: In the same ātman there is no difference of categories like man, god etc. But it is the false identification of ātman with the body (of man, god etc.) which causes dissatisfaction.
In the later half: Bhāgavata Candrikā prefers to read lokāḥ for loke. Lokas i.e. sentient beings are different (as men, gods etc) as a consequence of their past karmas.
(3) Padaratnāvalī affirms that there is a difference in the world as friend or foe; but it is due to misapprehension that dissatisfaction or troubles to mind are caused. People are different from each other, according to their past actions (karmas).

devanagari: परितुष्येत् ततस्तात तावन्मात्रेण पूरुषः । दैवोपसादितं यावद् वीक्ष्येश्वरगतिं बुधः ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: parituṣyet tatastāta tāvanmātreṇa pūruṣaḥdaivopasāditaṃ yāvad vīkṣyeśvaragatiṃ budhaḥ
translation: Therefore, observing the ways of the Almighty, a wise man should remain contented with whatever has been allotted to him by the Providence, Oh child.

devanagari: अथ मात्रोपदिष्टेन योगेनावरुरुत्ससि । यत्प्रसादं स वै पुंसां दुराराध्यो मतो मम ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: atha mātropadiṣṭena yogenāvarurutsasiyatprasādaṃ sa vai puṃsāṃ durārādhyo mato mama
translation: Moreover, the Lord whose grace you aspire after by the path of Yoga as advised by your mother, is in my opinion, very difficult to be propitiated by men.

devanagari: मुनयः पदवीं यस्य निःसङ्गेनोरुजन्मभिः । न विदुर्मृगयन्तोऽपि तीव्रयोगसमाधिना ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: munayaḥ padavīṃ yasya niḥsaṅgenorujanmabhiḥna vidurmṛgayanto'pi tīvrayogasamādhinā
translation: Though the sages have been seeking his path by intense Yogic meditation, and by remaining free from attachment through many births, they have not been able to find it.

devanagari: अतो निवर्ततामेष निर्बन्धस्तव निष्फलः । यतिष्यति भवान् काले श्रेयसां समुपस्थिते ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: ato nivartatāmeṣa nirbandhastava niṣphalaḥyatiṣyati bhavān kāle śreyasāṃ samupasthite
translation: Hence please return home. This persistence of yours is futile. You may try for your good (or final beatitude), at the proper time (viz. in advanced age).

devanagari: यस्य यद् दैवविहितं स तेन सुखदुःखयोः । आत्मानं तोषयन् देही तमसः पारमृच्छति ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: yasya yad daivavihitaṃ sa tena sukhaduḥkhayoḥātmānaṃ toṣayan dehī tamasaḥ pāramṛcchati
translation: An embodied being, who reconciles himself to the pleasure or pain (happiness or misery) meted out to him by Providence, reaches the end of the darkness of ignorance (i.e. attains Mokṣa).

devanagari: गुणाधिकान्मुदं लिप्सेद् अनुक्रोशं गुणाधमात् । मैत्रीं समानादन्विच्छेत् न तापैरभिभूयते ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: guṇādhikānmudaṃ lipsed anukrośaṃ guṇādhamātmaitrīṃ samānādanvicchet na tāpairabhibhūyate
translation: One should feel delighted to see a person of superior merits; should feel compassion to one who is inferior; and should desire friendship with one’s equals. (In this way) one is not overwhelmed with afflictions.

devanagari: ध्रुव उवाच । सोऽयं शमो भगवता सुखदुःखहतात्मनाम् । दर्शितः कृपया पुंसां दुर्दर्शोऽस्मद्विधैस्तु यः ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: dhruva uvācaso'yaṃ śamo bhagavatā sukhaduḥkhahatātmanāmdarśitaḥ kṛpayā puṃsāṃ durdarśo'smadvidhaistu yaḥ
translation:
Dhruva said:
This path of tranquillity has been graciously shown by your venerable Self, to persons whose hearts are affected with pleasure and pain. But it is beyond the ken of persons like us.

devanagari: अथापि मेऽविनीतस्य क्षात्त्रं घोरमुपेयुषः । सुरुच्या दुर्वचोबाणैः न भिन्ने श्रयते हृदि ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: athāpi me'vinītasya kṣāttraṃ ghoramupeyuṣaḥsurucyā durvacobāṇaiḥ na bhinne śrayate hṛdi
translation: It, however, does not dwell (i.e. impress) in my heart which is shattered by sharp arrow-like words of Suruci—heart of mine who am undisciplined, and have inherited terrible martial (Kṣatriya) spirit.

devanagari: पदं त्रिभुवनोत्कृष्टं जिगीषोः साधु वर्त्म मे । ब्रूहि अस्मत् पितृभिर्ब्रह्मन् अन्यैरप्यनधिष्ठितम् ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: padaṃ tribhuvanotkṛṣṭaṃ jigīṣoḥ sādhu vartma mebrūhi asmat pitṛbhirbrahman anyairapyanadhiṣṭhitam
translation: Oh Brāhmaṇa (sage Nārada), I desire to secure (lit. conquer) a place which is the highest and the most exalted of all in the worlds, and which had never been attained either by forefathers or anybody else. Be pleased to tell me a good path (leading to it).

devanagari: नूनं भवान् भगवतो योऽङ्गजः परमेष्ठिनः । वितुदन्नटते वीणां हिताय जगतोऽर्कवत् ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: nūnaṃ bhavān bhagavato yo'ṅgajaḥ parameṣṭhinaḥvitudannaṭate vīṇāṃ hitāya jagato'rkavat
translation: You are the son of the Lord Parameṣṭhin (Brahmā). Certainly it is for welfare of the world, that your honour roam over (the world) like the Sun, playing on (your) Vīṇā (lute).

devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । इत्युदाहृतमाकर्ण्य भगवान् नारदस्तदा । प्रीतः प्रत्याह तं बालं सद्वाक्यं अनुकम्पया ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: maitreya uvācaityudāhṛtamākarṇya bhagavān nāradastadāprītaḥ pratyāha taṃ bālaṃ sadvākyaṃ anukampayā
translation:
Maitreya said:
Hearing this excellent reply, the venerable Nārada was pleased with the child. Out of compassion, he then spoke to him the (following) excellent words in reply.

devanagari: नारद उवाच । जनन्याभिहितः पन्थाः स वै निःश्रेयसस्य ते । भगवान् वासुदेवस्तं भज तं प्रवणात्मना ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: nārada uvācajananyābhihitaḥ panthāḥ sa vai niḥśreyasasya tebhagavān vāsudevastaṃ bhaja taṃ pravaṇātmanā
translation:
Nārada said:
The path pointed out to you by your mother will verily lead to the highest good. The course is no other than Lord Vāsudeva. Adore him with a mind completely devoted to him.

devanagari: धर्मार्थकाममोक्षाख्यं य इच्छेत् श्रेय आत्मनः । एकं ह्येव हरेस्तत्र कारणं पादसेवनम् ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: dharmārthakāmamokṣākhyaṃ ya icchet śreya ātmanaḥekaṃ hyeva harestatra kāraṇaṃ pādasevanam
translation: If one wishes to be blest with good fortune known as Dharma, Artha, Kāma and Mokṣa (righteousness, wealth, sense-pleasure and Liberation from Saṃsāra), worship of Hari’s feet, is the only course for attaining it.

devanagari: तत्तात गच्छ भद्रं ते यमुनायास्तटं शुचि । पुण्यं मधुवनं यत्र सान्निध्यं नित्यदा हरेः ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: tattāta gaccha bhadraṃ te yamunāyāstaṭaṃ śucipuṇyaṃ madhuvanaṃ yatra sānnidhyaṃ nityadā hareḥ
translation: Therefore, Oh Child, God bless you! You go to the holy bank of the Yamunā where there is the sacred Madhuvana which is ever permeated with Hari’s presence.

devanagari: स्नात्वानुसवनं तस्मिन् कालिन्द्याः सलिले शिवे । कृत्वोचितानि निवसन् आत्मनः कल्पितासनः ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: snātvānusavanaṃ tasmin kālindyāḥ salile śivekṛtvocitāni nivasan ātmanaḥ kalpitāsanaḥ
translation: You bathe in the holy waters of the Yamunā three times (morning, mid-day and evening) a day; perform religious duties (e.g. bowing to god) proper for (a child like) you; be steady in a suitable easy posture (like Svastikāsana) on a seat (of Kuśa-grass etc.).

devanagari: प्राणायामेन त्रिवृता प्राणेन्द्रियमनोमलम् । शनैर्व्युदस्याभिध्यायेन् मनसा गुरुणा गुरुम् ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: prāṇāyāmena trivṛtā prāṇendriyamanomalamśanairvyudasyābhidhyāyen manasā guruṇā gurum
translation: Gradually remove the impurities fickleness etc. of Prāṇa, Indriyas (sense-organs) and the mind by threefold Prāṇāyāma (breath-control consisting of Pūraka, Kumbhaka and Recaka). With a steady and a  concentrated mind, you should meditate upon Lord Hari.

devanagari: प्रसादाभिमुखं शश्वत् प्रसन्नवदनेक्षणम् । सुनासं सुभ्रुवं चारु कपोलं सुरसुन्दरम् ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: prasādābhimukhaṃ śaśvat prasannavadanekṣaṇamsunāsaṃ subhruvaṃ cāru kapolaṃ surasundaram
translation: Hari is disposed to show his grace. His face and eyes are ever graciously cheerful. He has a well-shaped nose, beautiful browsand charming cheeks. He is the most beautiful of all gods.

devanagari: तरुणं रमणीयाङ्गं अरुणोष्ठेक्षणाधरम् । प्रणताश्रयणं नृम्णं शरण्यं करुणार्णवम् ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: taruṇaṃ ramaṇīyāṅgaṃ aruṇoṣṭhekṣaṇādharampraṇatāśrayaṇaṃ nṛmṇaṃ śaraṇyaṃ karuṇārṇavam
translation: He is ever youthful with beautiful limbs, red lips and reddish eyes. He is the only resort of all suppliants (devotees), the dispenser of infinite happiness (or the reservoir of all Puruṣārthas, the protector and the ocean of mercy.

devanagari: श्रीवत्साङ्कं घनश्यामं पुरुषं वनमालिनम् । शङ्खचक्रगदापद्मैः अभिव्यक्तचतुर्भुजम् ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīvatsāṅkaṃ ghanaśyāmaṃ puruṣaṃ vanamālinamśaṅkhacakragadāpadmaiḥ abhivyaktacaturbhujam
translation: He is distinguished by the mark called Śrī-Vatsa. He possesses all the characteristics of the Supreme Man. He is dark blue like a cloud, and wears a Vanamālā (about his neck); and his four arms are distinguished with Śaṅkha (a conch), Cakra (a discus), Gadā (a mace) and Padma (a lotus).

devanagari: किरीटिनं कुण्डलिनं केयूरवलयान्वितम् । कौस्तुभाभरणग्रीवं पीतकौशेयवाससम् ॥ ४८ ॥
english_devnagari: kirīṭinaṃ kuṇḍalinaṃ keyūravalayānvitamkaustubhābharaṇagrīvaṃ pītakauśeyavāsasam
translation: He is adorned with a crown, (a pair of) ear-rings, armlets and bracelets. His neck adds to the beauty of the Kaustubha gem. He wears yellow silken garments.

devanagari: काञ्चीकलापपर्यस्तं लसत्काञ्चन नूपुरम् । दर्शनीयतमं शान्तं मनोनयनवर्धनम् ॥ ४९ ॥
english_devnagari: kāñcīkalāpaparyastaṃ lasatkāñcana nūpuramdarśanīyatamaṃ śāntaṃ manonayanavardhanam
translation: He wears a girdle furnished with small tinkling bells round his waist, and is adorned with anklets of burnished gold. He is extremely beautiful, serene, and enhancer of the delight of the mind and the eyes.

devanagari: पद्‍भ्यां नखमणिश्रेण्या विलसद्‍भ्यां समर्चताम् । हृत्पद्मकर्णिकाधिष्ण्यं आक्रम्यात् मन्यवस्थितम् ॥ ५० ॥
english_devnagari: pad‍bhyāṃ nakhamaṇiśreṇyā vilasad‍bhyāṃ samarcatāmhṛtpadmakarṇikādhiṣṇyaṃ ākramyāt manyavasthitam
translation: Having occupied the pericarp of the lotus in the form of his devotees’ heart, with his feet beautified by a row of gem like nails, he presents himself to the mind of his worshippers.

devanagari: स्मयमानं अभिध्यायेत् सानुरागावलोकनम् । नियतेनैकभूतेन मनसा वरदर्षभम् ॥ ५१ ॥
english_devnagari: smayamānaṃ abhidhyāyet sānurāgāvalokanamniyatenaikabhūtena manasā varadarṣabham
translation: With a steady, concentrated mind, one should contemplate Hari, the formost among the bestowers of boons, as (having a) smiling (face) and looking with affection (to the devotees).

devanagari: एवं भगवतो रूपं सुभद्रं ध्यायतो मनः । निर्वृत्या परया तूर्णं सम्पन्नं न निवर्तते ॥ ५२ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ bhagavato rūpaṃ subhadraṃ dhyāyato manaḥnirvṛtyā parayā tūrṇaṃ sampannaṃ na nivartate
translation: The mind which contemplates the extremely auspicious form of the Lord in this way, is soon filled with highest bliss[4] and does not revert (to saṃsāra).
purport:
Footnote [4]:
v.l. nivṛtti: renunciation.‘The mind is immediately filled with renunciation (of worldly objects)’.

devanagari: जपश्च परमो गुह्यः श्रूयतां मे नृपात्मज । यं सप्तरात्रं प्रपठन् पुमान् पश्यति खेचरान् ॥ ५३ ॥
english_devnagari: japaśca paramo guhyaḥ śrūyatāṃ me nṛpātmajayaṃ saptarātraṃ prapaṭhan pumān paśyati khecarān
translation: Oh Prince, hear from me the most secret mantra (mystic formula to be continuously repeated mentally during meditation). Any person who repeats it for seven days (and nights) can see the heavenly beings (moving through the skies).

devanagari: ॐ नमो भगवते वासुदेवाय । मन्त्रेणानेन देवस्य कुर्याद् द्रव्यमयीं बुधः । सपर्यां विविधैर्द्रव्यैः देशकालविभागवित् ॥ ५४ ॥
english_devnagari: oṃ namo bhagavate vāsudevāyamantreṇānena devasya kuryād dravyamayīṃ budhaḥsaparyāṃ vividhairdravyaiḥ deśakālavibhāgavit
translation: The Mantra is “oṃ namo bhagavate vāsudevāya” (salutation to venerable Vāsudeva who is the embodiment of OM or praṇava). Judging the appropriateness of the articles of worship with due regard to time and place, a wise man should perform the worship of the Lord with various material substances, repeating all the while this Mantra.

devanagari: सलिलैः शुचिभिर्माल्यैः वन्यैर्मूलफलादिभिः । शस्ताङ्कुरांशुकैश्चार्चेत् तुलस्या प्रियया प्रभुम् ॥ ५५ ॥
english_devnagari: salilaiḥ śucibhirmālyaiḥ vanyairmūlaphalādibhiḥśastāṅkurāṃśukaiścārcet tulasyā priyayā prabhum
translation: He should worship the Lord with sacred waters, garland of flowers, roots, fruits etc. found in forests, sprouts approved for worship (e.g. durvā-grass-blades), garments and leaves etc. of Tulasī so dear to the Lord.

devanagari: लब्ध्वा द्रव्यमयीमर्चां क्षित्यम्ब्वादिषु वार्चयेत् । आभृतात्मा मुनिः शान्तो यतवाङ्मितवन्यभुक् ॥ ५६ ॥
english_devnagari: labdhvā dravyamayīmarcāṃ kṣityambvādiṣu vārcayetābhṛtātmā muniḥ śānto yatavāṅmitavanyabhuk
translation: Having secured an image of substances like stone or through the medium of the earth, water etc. as a substitute for the image, the sage should perform the worship of the Lord. He should be controlled in mind[5] and speech; should be serene and should subsist on moderate quantity of forest products (life fruit, roots etc.).
purport:
Footnote [5]:
v.l. ādṛtātmā—with a devout mind—Bhāgavata Candrikā
āhṛtātmā—One who has controlled his mind by withdrawing it from sensual pleasure.

devanagari: स्वेच्छावतारचरितैः अचिन्त्यनिजमायया । करिष्यति उत्तमश्लोकः तद् ध्यायेद् हृदयङ्गमम् ॥ ५७ ॥
english_devnagari: svecchāvatāracaritaiḥ acintyanijamāyayākariṣyati uttamaślokaḥ tad dhyāyed hṛdayaṅgamam
translation: He should meditate upon the glorious deeds which the Lord of hallowing fame, will perform in the incarnations which He assumes at his will and pleasure, by the force of his incomprehensible Māyā.

devanagari: परिचर्या भगवतो यावत्यः पूर्वसेविताः । ता मंत्रहृदयेनैव प्रयुञ्ज्यान् मंत्रमूर्तये ॥ ५८ ॥
english_devnagari: paricaryā bhagavato yāvatyaḥ pūrvasevitāḥtā maṃtrahṛdayenaiva prayuñjyān maṃtramūrtaye
translation: Whatever articles (or forms) of worship are adopted by the ancients for his adoration, should be offered to the Lord embodied in the Mantra, by means of repeating the same Mantra (of twelve letters).

devanagari: एवं कायेन मनसा वचसा च मनोगतम् । परिचर्यमाणो भगवान् भक्तिमत्परिचर्यया ॥ ५९ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ kāyena manasā vacasā ca manogatamparicaryamāṇo bhagavān bhaktimatparicaryayā
translation: When the Lord is thus being worshipped with body, speech and mind with acts of devotion, he enhances the Divine Love in men who properly perform his devotion sincerely. He confers upon them the bliss cherished by them as beneficial, in the form of Dharma, Artha etc.

devanagari: पुंसां अमायिनां सम्यक् भजतां भाववर्धनः । श्रेयो दिशत्यभिमतं यद्धर्मादिषु देहिनाम् ॥ ६० ॥
english_devnagari: puṃsāṃ amāyināṃ samyak bhajatāṃ bhāvavardhanaḥśreyo diśatyabhimataṃ yaddharmādiṣu dehinām
translation: When the Lord is thus being worshipped with body, speech and mind with acts of devotion, he enhances the Divine Love in men who properly perform his devotion sincerely. He confers upon them the bliss cherished by them as beneficial, in the form of Dharma, Artha etc.

devanagari: विरक्तश्चेन्द्रियरतौ भक्तियोगेन भूयसा । तं निरन्तरभावेन भजेताद्धा विमुक्तये ॥ ६१ ॥
english_devnagari: viraktaścendriyaratau bhaktiyogena bhūyasātaṃ nirantarabhāvena bhajetāddhā vimuktaye
translation: He (the devotee) should be indifferent to sensual pleasures. With a highly intensive practice of Bhakti-yoga (path of devotion) and with his mind exclusively devoted to God, he should worship him directly for Liberation from Saṃsāra.

devanagari: इत्युक्तस्तं परिक्रम्य प्रणम्य च नृपार्भकः । ययौ मधुवनं पुण्यं हरेश्चरणचर्चितम् ॥ ६२ ॥
english_devnagari: ityuktastaṃ parikramya praṇamya ca nṛpārbhakaḥyayau madhuvanaṃ puṇyaṃ hareścaraṇacarcitam
translation: The Prince, who was thus advised, circumambulated Nārada. After bowing down to him (Nārada), he went to the holy Madhuvana which was adorned with Hari’s footprints.

devanagari: तपोवनं गते तस्मिन् प्रविष्टोऽन्तःपुरं मुनिः । अर्हितार्हणको राज्ञा सुखासीन उवाच तम् ॥ ६३ ॥
english_devnagari: tapovanaṃ gate tasmin praviṣṭo'ntaḥpuraṃ muniḥarhitārhaṇako rājñā sukhāsīna uvāca tam
translation: When Dhruva went to the forest for penance, the sage Nārada entered (Uttānapāda’s) harem. He was received with due worship by the king. Seating himself comfortably, he spoke to the king.

devanagari: नारद उवाच । राजन् किं ध्यायसे दीर्घं मुखेन परिशुष्यता । किं वा न रिष्यते कामो धर्मो वार्थेन संयुतः ॥ ६४ ॥
english_devnagari: nārada uvācarājan kiṃ dhyāyase dīrghaṃ mukhena pariśuṣyatākiṃ vā na riṣyate kāmo dharmo vārthena saṃyutaḥ
translation:
Nārada said:
Oh King, what is it that you think about so long, with a withering face? Is there any deficiency in your Dharma (religious duties), Artha (wealth and prosperity) and kāma (fulfillment of desires)?

devanagari: राजोवाच । सुतो मे बालको ब्रह्मन् स्त्रैणेनाकरुणात्मना । निर्वासितः पञ्चवर्षः सह मात्रा महान्कविः ॥ ६५ ॥
english_devnagari: rājovācasuto me bālako brahman straiṇenākaruṇātmanānirvāsitaḥ pañcavarṣaḥ saha mātrā mahānkaviḥ
translation:
The King replied:
Oh sage My wise and noble son of five years has been exiled along with his mother by me, who became heartless due to my submissiveness to (her) co-wife.

devanagari: अप्यनाथं वने ब्रह्मन् मा स्मादन्त्यर्भकं वृकाः । श्रान्तं शयानं क्षुधितं परिम्लानमुखाम्बुजम् ॥ ६६ ॥
english_devnagari: apyanāthaṃ vane brahman mā smādantyarbhakaṃ vṛkāḥśrāntaṃ śayānaṃ kṣudhitaṃ parimlānamukhāmbujam
translation: Oh Sage May not the wolves prey upon my helpless child lying in the forest, fatigued and famished, with his lotuslike face faded.

devanagari: अहो मे बत दौरात्म्यं स्त्रीजितस्योपधारय । योऽङ्कं प्रेम्णाऽरुरुक्षन्तं नाभ्यनन्दमसत्तमः ॥ ६७ ॥
english_devnagari: aho me bata daurātmyaṃ strījitasyopadhārayayo'ṅkaṃ premṇā'rurukṣantaṃ nābhyanandamasattamaḥ
translation: What a pity! Look at the wickedness of a fellow like me who am under the thumb of a women. I am an extremely vile fellow, I did not welcome him while he affectionately wished to climb my lap.

devanagari: नारद उवाच । मा मा शुचः स्वतनयं देवगुप्तं विशाम्पते । तत्प्रभावं अविज्ञाय प्रावृङ्क्ते यद्यशो जगत् ॥ ६८ ॥
english_devnagari: nārada uvācamā mā śucaḥ svatanayaṃ devaguptaṃ viśāmpatetatprabhāvaṃ avijñāya prāvṛṅkte yadyaśo jagat
translation:
Nārada said:
Oh King! Do not worry about your child at all. He is protected by the Lord. You do not know his greatness. His glory will spread over the whole world.

devanagari: सुदुष्करं कर्म कृत्वा लोकपालैरपि प्रभुः । ऐष्यत्यचिरतो राजन् यशो विपुलयंस्तव ॥ ६९ ॥
english_devnagari: suduṣkaraṃ karma kṛtvā lokapālairapi prabhuḥaiṣyatyacirato rājan yaśo vipulayaṃstava
translation: Oh King! After achieving a feat difficult even for the Protectors of the world, your powerful son will soon return enhancing your glory.

devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । इति देवर्षिणा प्रोक्तं विश्रुत्य जगतीपतिः । राजलक्ष्मीमनादृत्य पुत्रं एवान्वचिन्तयत् ॥ ७० ॥
english_devnagari: maitreya uvācaiti devarṣiṇā proktaṃ viśrutya jagatīpatiḥrājalakṣmīmanādṛtya putraṃ evānvacintayat
translation:
Maitreya said:
Having thus heard the speech of the divine sage Nārada, the King Uttānapāda discarded his royal fortune and anxiously brooded over his son only.

devanagari: तत्राभिषिक्तः प्रयतः तां उपोष्य विभावरीम् । समाहितः पर्यचर दृष्यादेशेन पूरुषम् ॥ ७१ ॥
english_devnagari: tatrābhiṣiktaḥ prayataḥ tāṃ upoṣya vibhāvarīmsamāhitaḥ paryacara dṛṣyādeśena pūruṣam
translation: There (in Madhuvana), Dhruva had his bath in the Yamunā. Remaining self-controlled, he fasted that night. With a concentrated mind, he worshipped the Supreme Man according to the instructions of the sage.

devanagari: त्रिरात्रान्ते त्रिरात्रान्ते कपित्थबदराशनः । आत्मवृत्त्यनुसारेण मासं निन्येऽर्चयन् हरिम् ॥ ७२ ॥
english_devnagari: trirātrānte trirātrānte kapitthabadarāśanaḥātmavṛttyanusāreṇa māsaṃ ninye'rcayan harim
translation: He passed one month in the worship of Hari, eating at the end of every third night fruits called kapittha (woodapple) and badara (jujub), just enough to maintain his life.

devanagari: द्वितीयं च तथा मासं षष्ठे षष्ठेऽर्भको दिने । तृणपर्णादिभिः शीर्णैः कृतान्नोऽभ्यर्चयन् विभुम् ॥ ७३ ॥
english_devnagari: dvitīyaṃ ca tathā māsaṃ ṣaṣṭhe ṣaṣṭhe'rbhako dinetṛṇaparṇādibhiḥ śīrṇaiḥ kṛtānno'bhyarcayan vibhum
translation: The second month also he continued the worship of the Lord, eating every sixth day withered blades of grass, leaves etc.

devanagari: तृतीयं चानयन् मासं नवमे नवमेऽहनि । अब्भक्ष उत्तमश्लोकं उपाधावत्समाधिना ॥ ७४ ॥
english_devnagari: tṛtīyaṃ cānayan māsaṃ navame navame'haniabbhakṣa uttamaślokaṃ upādhāvatsamādhinā
translation: He spent the third month in propitiating the Lord of hallowing fame, with perfectly concentrated mind, drinking water only, on every ninth day.

devanagari: चतुर्थमपि वै मासं द्वादशे द्वादशेऽहनि । वायुभक्षो जितश्वासो ध्यायन् देवमधारयत् ॥ ७५ ॥
english_devnagari: caturthamapi vai māsaṃ dvādaśe dvādaśe'hanivāyubhakṣo jitaśvāso dhyāyan devamadhārayat
translation: With perfect control over his breath, he passed the fourth month in contemplating the Lord, subsisting on the air, on every twelfth day.

devanagari: पञ्चमे मास्यनुप्राप्ते जितश्वासो नृपात्मजः । ध्यायन् ब्रह्म पदैकेन तस्थौ स्थाणुरिवाचलः ॥ ७६ ॥
english_devnagari: pañcame māsyanuprāpte jitaśvāso nṛpātmajaḥdhyāyan brahma padaikena tasthau sthāṇurivācalaḥ
translation: When the fifth month arrived, the Prince controlled his life-breath and meditating upon Brahman (the Supreme being) stood motionless like a post.

devanagari: सर्वतो मन आकृष्य हृदि भूतेन्द्रियाशयम् । ध्यायन् भगवतो रूपं नाद्राक्षीत् किंचनापरम् ॥ ७७ ॥
english_devnagari: sarvato mana ākṛṣya hṛdi bhūtendriyāśayamdhyāyan bhagavato rūpaṃ nādrākṣīt kiṃcanāparam
translation: He withdrew his mind which is the basis of the sense-organs and their objects from every side (external objects). He meditated upon the form of the Lord in his heart and perceived nothing else.

devanagari: आधारं महदादीनां प्रधानपुरुषेश्वरम् । ब्रह्म धारयमाणस्य त्रयो लोकाश्चकम्पिरे ॥ ७८ ॥
english_devnagari: ādhāraṃ mahadādīnāṃ pradhānapuruṣeśvarambrahma dhārayamāṇasya trayo lokāścakampire
translation: While he was having the Dhāraṇā (steadfast fixation of mind on the Supreme Brahman, the support of Mahat and other principles and the controller of Pradhāna and Puruṣa, all the three worlds began to shake.

devanagari: यदैकपादेन स पार्थिवार्भकः । तस्थौ तदङ्गुष्ठनिपीडिता मही । ननाम तत्रार्धमिभेन्द्रधिष्ठिता । तरीव सव्येतरतः पदे पदे ॥ ७९ ॥
english_devnagari: yadaikapādena sa pārthivārbhakaḥtasthau tadaṅguṣṭhanipīḍitā mahīnanāma tatrārdhamibhendradhiṣṭhitātarīva savyetarataḥ pade pade
translation: When the child of the King (i.e. Dhruva) stood on one leg, the earth, being pressed down by his great toe, became semi-inclined to one side, as a ferry-boat carrying a big elephant does at every step, according as he raises his right or left leg.

devanagari: तस्मिन् अभिध्यायति विश्वमात्मनो । द्वारं निरुध्यासुमनन्यया धिया । लोका निरुच्छ्वासनिपीडिता भृशं । सलोकपालाः शरणं ययुर्हरिम् ॥ ८० ॥
english_devnagari: tasmin abhidhyāyati viśvamātmanodvāraṃ nirudhyāsumananyayā dhiyālokā nirucchvāsanipīḍitā bhṛśaṃsalokapālāḥ śaraṇaṃ yayurharim
translation: Dhruva controlled his life-breath and the (working) of) his senses and began to meditate on Hari, the Soul of the universe as not being different from himself. While he was contemplating thus, the worlds along with their protecting deities became greatly distressed at the stoppage of their breath, and they resorted to Hari for refuge.

devanagari: देवा ऊचुः । नैवं विदामो भगवन् प्राणरोधं । चराचरस्याखिलसत्त्वधाम्नः । विधेहि तन्नो वृजिनाद्विमोक्षं । प्राप्ता वयं त्वां शरणं शरण्यम् ॥ ८...
english_devnagari: devā ūcuḥnaivaṃ vidāmo bhagavan prāṇarodhaṃcarācarasyākhilasattvadhāmnaḥvidhehi tanno vṛjinādvimokṣaṃprāptā vayaṃ tvāṃ śaraṇaṃ śaraṇyam
translation:
Gods said:
We do not know the reason of this sudden and simultaneous stoppage of breath of all beings, mobile and immobile. We have sought refuge under you who alone can afford shelter to us. Therefore save us from this calamity.

devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच । मा भैष्ट बालं तपसो दुरत्ययान् । निवर्तयिष्ये प्रतियात स्वधाम । यतो हि वः प्राणनिरोध आसीत् । औत्तानपादिर्मयि सङ्गतात्मा ॥...
english_devnagari: śrībhagavānuvācamā bhaiṣṭa bālaṃ tapaso duratyayānnivartayiṣye pratiyāta svadhāmayato hi vaḥ prāṇanirodha āsītauttānapādirmayi saṅgatātmā
translation:
The Lord said:
Do not be afraid. I shall dissuade the child (Dhruva) from this austere penance. You return to your respective abodes. The son of King Uttānapāda has identified his Self with me (as the Soul ofthe universe). This has led to the stoppage of your breath.
[back to top]
